content: Biological and chemical pathogenic agents (from Topic A's classical definition) naturally follow the core parallel orchestration pattern of reader agents defined in Topic B: each individual pathogenic agent acts as an independent reader that ingests and parses the host's physiological microenvironment in parallel, while the overall pathogenic cascade is orchestrated by the collective agent population's distributed dynamic, rather than a single centralized regulator. This re-framing opens new cross-domain approaches to model both biological infection and multi-agent AI workflows.
source_topics: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
